title: Initialize
description: Method `initialize` (host → ext, request). The handshake: the host announces its `protocol_version`, identity, workspace + trust, session, frontend `mode`/UI capabilities, and which capability groups are enabled; the extension replies with the negotiated `protocol_version`, its identity, and its registrations (tools, commands, flags, event subscriptions). Both sides ignore unknown fields; the effective protocol version is `min(host, ext)`.

protocol_version.description: Highest SEP version the host speaks.

root.description: Absolute path to the active workspace root.

trusted.description: Whether the workspace is trusted. Project extensions load only when true.

session.description: Present when the host has a session concept (daemon/CLI); absent for the bare engine.

mode.description: Frontend rendering mode; drives which `ui/request` kinds are answerable.

ui_capabilities.description: UI request kinds the frontend can render, e.g. `select`, `confirm`, `input`, `notify`, `set_status`, `set_widget`, `set_title`. Empty ⇒ headless.

flags.description: Parsed values for the flags the extension declares (name → value). A host with a CLI surface fills this; hosts without one send it empty or omit it. The extension reads its flag values here at startup.

capabilities_enabled.description: Which host capability groups the extension may call. A method in a disabled group is rejected with -32004 CapabilityDisabled.

protocol_version.description: Highest SEP version the extension speaks. Effective version = min(host, ext).

Registrations.title: SepRegistrations
Registrations.description: What the extension contributes. Also the payload of a later `registry/update` notification.

flags.description: CLI/slash flag names the extension owns; the host delivers their parsed values in `initialize` params `flags`.

shortcuts.description: Keyboard shortcuts binding a chord to a registered command. Only frontends with a key surface (the TUI) honor them.

subscriptions.description: Event names the extension wants delivered. Doubles as the host's dispatch filter — unsubscribed events are never sent.

name.description: Bare tool name; the host exposes it as `<extension>.<name>`.

parameters.description: JSON Schema for the tool's arguments.

deferred.description: If true, register into the host's deferred (tool_search-promoted) tier instead of eager.

key.description: Human-typed chord, e.g. `ctrl+p` or `f2`; the frontend parses it.

command.description: The registered command name this chord invokes (no leading `/`).
